step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("definition and examples of cross-sectional study")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed evidence gathered, cross-sectional studies indeed focus on assessing varying individuals at a particular instant, capturing the state of affairs without extending follow-ups. The retrieved references consistently highlight that cross-sectional methodologies aim merely to obtain a snapshot of health or trait distributions among comparable groups at one point in time. They don’t track individuals over longer durations nor revisit them later—a stark contrast to longitudinal designs. Given this clarification and aligning strictly with option descriptions provided originally:
